title: TRAIL: Trace Reasoning and Agentic Issue Localization
problem: Agent traces are hard to debug because reasoning, planning, and execution errors interact across long trajectories.
limitation: Directly covers step-level trace issue localization; major novelty threat.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Maximum threat: covers trace reasoning, OTel-style traces, formal taxonomy, 148 traces, 841 errors.
verification_note: arXiv HTML and HuggingFace dataset pages confirm 148 traces, 841 errors, reasoning/planning/execution taxonomy.

title: AgentRx: Diagnosing AI Agent Failures from Execution Trajectories
problem: Failed agent trajectories require localizing the first unrecoverable critical failure step.
limitation: Very direct threat: covers constraint-based diagnosis and repair-adjacent validation logs.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Maximum threat: pinpoints critical failure step using constraints and auditable validation logs across tau-bench, Flash, Magentic-One.
verification_note: arXiv abstract and Microsoft Research blog confirm 115 annotated failed trajectories and critical failure step localization.

title: Seeing the Whole Elephant: A Benchmark for Failure Attribution in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
problem: Existing MAS attribution often lacks full execution observability needed for debugging.
metrics: responsible component accuracy; decisive step accuracy
limitation: Very direct threat: full execution traces, reproducible environments, counterfactual probing support.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Maximum threat: covers failure attribution with full execution traces and reproducible environments.
verification_note: ACL page confirms 220 annotated failure traces, full execution observability, and up to 76.5% attribution improvement.

title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When? On Automated Failure Attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
problem: MAS failures require identifying the responsible agent and decisive step.
limitation: Multi-agent focused; still directly covers step attribution.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Maximum threat: formalizes automated failure attribution for MAS and includes decisive error step labels.
verification_note: PMLR page confirms ICML 2025 paper, 127 systems, and step attribution results.

title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?
problem: MAS failures need empirically grounded categories and scalable judging.
limitation: Taxonomy focus rather than causal slice, but strong root-cause category threat.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Strong threat: 14 failure modes across specification, inter-agent misalignment, task verification.
verification_note: arXiv PDF search result confirms 14 failure modes and LLM-as-judge pipeline.

title: Where Agents Fail and How They Can Learn From Failures
problem: Agent failures cascade across memory, reflection, planning, action, and system modules.
metrics: all-correct accuracy; step accuracy; task success lift
limitation: Directly overlaps with root-cause isolation and corrective feedback.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Strong threat: AgentErrorTaxonomy, AgentErrorBench, and AgentDebug recover from failures.
verification_note: OpenReview PDF confirms taxonomy, annotated failure trajectories, and AgentDebug framework.

title: AgentTelemetry: A Fault Detection Benchmark and Toolkit for LLM Agent Observability
problem: Standard observability misses agent-specific planning, reasoning, delegation, and memory faults.
limitation: Observability focus; less about causal benchmark labels, but strong representation threat.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Strong representation threat: defines agent-specific span kinds and fault taxonomy.
verification_note: OpenReview PDF confirms 9 span kinds, 14 fault types, and SWE-bench Lite case study.

title: VeriLA: A Human-Centered Evaluation Framework for Interpretable Verification of LLM Agent Failures
problem: Agent failures are opaque and expensive for humans to inspect.
limitation: Workshop scope and verifier framing; relevant to repair guidance but not direct trace-causal benchmark.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Related threat: decomposes tasks and verifies agent outputs against human-defined criteria.
verification_note: Megagon and author pages confirm arXiv-linked VeriLA framework and CHI HEAL Workshop 2025.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
problem: LLMs must resolve real GitHub issues with patches that pass tests.
limitation: Final success benchmark; not a trace diagnosis dataset by itself.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Trace source and application domain for software-agent failure localization.
verification_note: OpenReview PDF confirms 2294 GitHub issue tasks and test-based evaluation.

title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
problem: General assistants require reasoning, browsing, multimodality, and tool use.
limitation: Does not provide failure localization by default.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Trace source for information-retrieval and tool-use agent failures.
verification_note: arXiv page confirms 466 questions and general assistant benchmark.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
problem: LLMs need evaluation as agents across diverse interactive environments.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Trace source / background benchmark for agent failure categories.
verification_note: OpenReview confirms ICLR 2024 benchmark across 8 environments.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
problem: Web agents need realistic long-horizon interaction tasks.
limitation: Not a failure diagnosis benchmark by default.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Potential trace source for web-agent causal failure localization.
verification_note: arXiv page confirms self-hostable web environment and benchmark.

title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
problem: Computer-use agents need real OS interaction and execution-based evaluation.
limitation: GUI/desktop focus; trace attribution not primary.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Potential trace source for multimodal agent failures.
verification_note: Official project page confirms 369 tasks and execution-based evaluation.

title: MultiAgentBench: Evaluating the Collaboration and Competition of LLM agents
problem: MAS evaluation should include collaboration and competition dynamics.
limitation: Measures protocol performance more than causal failure repair targets.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Background multi-agent benchmark and source of coordination failures.
verification_note: ACL PDF confirms benchmark and MARBLE coordination protocols.

title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
problem: Agents must converse, use APIs, and follow policies consistently.
limitation: Policy/tool benchmark; diagnosis appears in AgentRx rather than tau-bench itself.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Useful trace source for structured API workflow failures.
verification_note: ICLR PDF confirms tau-bench and pass^k.

title: AppWorld: A Controllable World of Apps and People for Benchmarking Interactive Coding Agents
problem: API agents must solve app tasks without collateral state damage.
limitation: App/API focus; not trace-causal diagnosis by default.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Potential structured trace source and failure state oracle.
verification_note: ACL Anthology confirms AppWorld and state-based evaluation.

title: TheAgentCompany: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Consequential Real World Tasks
problem: Digital-worker agents need web, coding, program execution, and coworker communication.
limitation: Broad benchmark; expensive to use as MVP.
relevance_to_trace_causal_failure_locali: Potential realistic trace source and consequential failure setting.
verification_note: arXiv PDF confirms simulated company tasks and autonomous completion rates.
